tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal conviction on near-term directional movement, with a median of $76,899 (-0.2%) suggesting a largely flat outlook. The 80% confidence interval ($76,000-$77,927) is relatively tight at ~2.4%, indicating modest expected volatility over this 60-market, $6.7M volume sample.

Markets price a slight bullish tilt to $77,416 (+0.5%), though wider distribution across the interquartile range ($75,310-$78,956) reflects increased uncertainty one day further out. The 10th-90th spread widens to ~7.8%, suggesting traders are pricing in more tail risk as the forecast horizon extends.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets see WTI holding near current levels by end-April 2026, with a median forecast of $84/bbl (flat vs. spot). However, the wide distribution reflects significant uncertainty, with a 10% probability of prices below $65 and a 10% probability exceeding $114/bbl.

Markets price in a notable 14% decline to $72/bbl by end-June 2026, suggesting a pullback from current spot. The tighter distribution (10th-90th percentile of $57-$83) indicates more consensus around weakness, with three-quarters of outcomes concentrated between $64-$79/bbl.

The wide interquartile range ($4,331-$5,562) reflects substantial uncertainty, with the 90th percentile at $6,536 suggesting meaningful tail risk to the upside, while the 10th percentile at $4,200 indicates downside protection near current levels.
